class.name: NeighbourQuery2D
class.brief_description: Node that provides fast spatial neighbourhood queries over subscribed [Node2D] instances.
class.description: [NeighbourQuery2D] maintains a uniform spatial grid that is rebuilt at a configurable interval during physics processing. [Node2D] instances register themselves via [method subscribe] and can be queried by world position using [method get_next], [method get_next_first], [method get_next_random], [method get_all], and [method get_closest]. Subscribers outside the [member domain] rectangle are ignored and not placed in the grid. The domain can be visualised in the editor and at runtime via [member draw_domain].

method.name: subscribe
method.description: Registers [param node] with a collision [param layer] bitmask. If [param node] is already subscribed its entry is overwritten.

method.name: unsubscribe
method.description: Removes [param node] from the server so it is no longer returned by spatial queries. Safe to omit if the node is about to be freed — stale entries are cleaned up automatically upon the next refresh.

method.name: get_next
method.description: Returns the nearest subscribed [Node2D] to [param position] whose layer matches [param layer_mask]. Returns [code]null[/code] if no match is found. [param max_distance] limits the search radius in world units. [param min_distance] excludes subscribers closer than this value. [param exclude] optionally skips a specific subscriber.

method.name: get_next_first
method.description: Returns the first subscribed [Node2D] found within range of [param position] whose layer matches [param layer_mask]. Returns [code]null[/code] if no match is found. This is the fastest query function: it expands outward cell by cell and returns immediately on the first valid hit, without scanning for the closest result. The returned node is not guaranteed to be the nearest one. [param max_distance] limits the search radius in world units. [param min_distance] excludes subscribers closer than this value. [param exclude] optionally skips a specific subscriber.

method.name: get_next_random
method.description: Returns a random subscribed [Node2D] within range of [param position] whose layer matches [param layer_mask]. Returns [code]null[/code] if no match is found. [param max_distance] limits the search radius in world units. [param min_distance] excludes subscribers closer than this value. [param exclude] optionally skips a specific subscriber.

method.name: get_all
method.description: Returns an [Array] of [Node2D] references for all subscribers within [param max_distance] of [param position] whose layer matches [param layer_mask]. [param min_distance] excludes subscribers closer than this value. [param exclude] optionally skips a specific subscriber.

method.name: get_closest
method.description: Returns an [Array] of up to [param max_count] [Node2D] references for the nearest subscribers to [param position] whose layer matches [param layer_mask]. The array is not sorted by distance. [param max_distance] limits the search radius in world units. [param min_distance] excludes subscribers closer than this value. [param exclude] optionally skips a specific subscriber.

constants.constant: Heatmap shows how many times each grid cell was visited during query traversal (cell reads).
Heatmap shows how many queries originated from within each cell (query origin density).

members.member: Width and height of each spatial grid cell in world units. Should be somewhere around the average interaction radius of your queries.
Minimum time in seconds between grid rebuilds. Set to [code]0.0[/code] (default) to rebuild every physics frame. Higher values reduce CPU cost but queried results may be stale.

When [code]true[/code] (default), subscriber positions are read via [method Node2D.get_global_position]. Set to [code]false[/code] to use [method Node2D.get_position] (local space), which is significantly faster when all subscribers share the same parent and no global transform traversal is needed.
The world-space rectangle that defines the active area. Subscribers located outside this rectangle are ignored during each grid refresh and will not appear in query results.
When [code]true[/code], the server draws the [member domain] boundary and grid overlay using the Node2D._draw API. [b]Warning:[/b] This feature is intended for debugging only.

Interval in seconds between redraws of the query heatmap. Only has an effect when [member debug_draw_domain] is [code]true[/code]. Set to a negative value to disable heatmap drawing entirely. [b]Warning:[/b] This feature is intended for debugging only. Frequent redraws at low intervals are very expensive at scale and will noticeably impact performance. [b]Warning:[/b] Heatmap data is only collected in debug builds ([code]DEBUG_INFORMATION[/code]). The property exists in all builds but has no visual effect in release.

Controls what the heatmap visualises. [constant CELL_READS] shows how many times each grid cell was visited during query traversal. [constant QUERY_COUNTS] shows how many queries originated from within each cell. [b]Warning:[/b] Only available in debug builds!

Interval in seconds between emissions of the [signal debug_info] signal carrying the timing report. Set to a negative value to disable periodic reporting entirely. [b]Warning:[/b] This feature is intended for debugging only. Only available in debug builds ([code]DEBUG_INFORMATION[/code]).

signal.name: debug_info
signal.description: Emitted at various points during processing to expose internal timing and diagnostic data. [b]Warning:[/b] While the signal is available in all builds most information is only processed in debug builds.
